summary: Iter 44 — P2 testing for 4 backlog items: (1) Transaction CRUD activity logs, (2) ActivityLogsPage date-range filter, (3) EmployeePerformancePage monthly/yearly breakdown recharts, (4) Resend weekly digest + APScheduler Monday 9:00 TR. Backend pytest: 9/9 PASS (tx create/update/delete/permanent-delete logs with correct details.changes shape; activity-logs ?start_date/?end_date filter incl. empty-range 2000 case; /api/stats/sales-breakdown monthly=12-bucket + yearly ≤5 buckets with label/sold_count/revenue/profit; /api/digest/preview admin 200 html>2000 chars + 403 for satis; /api/digest/send-now admin 200 sent=true + 403 for satis). APScheduler startup log 'Weekly digest scheduler started (mon 9:00)' confirmed in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. Frontend: ActivityLogsPage date filter inputs work (API call to /api/activity-logs?start_date&end_date verified); EmployeePerformancePage renders recharts BarChart (85 svg g elements), monthly/yearly toggle works, year-select hides on yearly. Settings DigestPanel: preview iframe now opens + send-now returns real Resend email_id (after bug fix below).

issues: logs-filter-clear button is conditionally rendered (only visible when at least one filter has a value). Acceptable UX, but spec said 'should appear'. Not blocking.

passed_tests: GET /api/activity-logs?start_date=YYYY-MM-DD&end_date=YYYY-MM-DD → filters created_at with $gte/$lte correctly; 2000-range returns count=0
GET /api/digest/preview admin → 200 with {stats, html>2000 chars}; stats has sold_count/new_car_count/price_change_count/stock_count
APScheduler startup log confirms 'Weekly digest scheduler started (mon 9:00)' + cron job registered
Frontend ActivityLogsPage: logs-filter-start/logs-filter-end inputs present; setting range triggers API call with start_date & end_date params

Frontend EmployeePerformancePage: perf-breakdown-chart + perf-period-monthly + perf-period-yearly + perf-year-select all render; recharts BarChart renders 85 svg g nodes; yearly toggle hides year-select

Frontend Settings DigestPanel: digest-panel + digest-preview-btn + digest-send-now-btn visible; preview-btn opens digest-preview-iframe; send-now returns digest-result='Gönderildi' with real Resend email_id (d6e80c8d-96a4-4cb8-bf3d-edf515210541)
action_items: OPTIONAL (UX): Consider making logs-filter-clear permanently visible (disabled when no filters active) for consistency — current implementation hides it entirely when filters are empty, which is acceptable but differs from spec literal wording.
OPTIONAL: DigestPanel axios error toast 'Önizleme alınamadı' has no details (line 45). Consider logging e.response?.data?.detail to help debugging.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: /app/frontend/src/components/settings/DigestPanel.jsx:15 used localStorage.getItem('token') but the app stores JWT under key 'crm_token' (set by LoginPage/AppContext/SSOCallbackPage). This caused 401 'Invalid token' for both Preview and Test Gönder in the frontend — backend was working correctly, so backend pytest passed. Fixed in-place to getItem('crm_token'). After fix, preview iframe opens correctly and send-now returns real email_id.

routes/digest.py:216 — current_user.get('role','admin') default 'admin' is a security surface: any user missing role field bypasses the 403 check. Since JWT injects role explicitly and startup migration sets role=admin for missing rows, practically safe, but defensive coding should default to a non-admin sentinel like 'satis' OR raise if role missing.

routes/digest.py:22 — SENDER_EMAIL fallback to 'onboarding@resend.dev' is fine for dev but production should fail-fast if SENDER_EMAIL env var is missing rather than silently use Resend sandbox sender.

routes/digest.py:160 — owner lookup uses {$or:[{id:org_id},{org_id, role:admin}]} which returns the first admin found; if multiple admins exist in an org only one (non-deterministic) gets the digest. Consider: fetch all admins and send digest to each, OR explicitly choose the owner (id==org_id).
routes/stats.py:167 — get_sales_breakdown uses _dt.utcnow() (naive) — consider datetime.now(timezone.utc).year for consistency; works fine now but deprecation warning in py3.12+.

routes/activity_logs.py:42 — end_date upper bound is '<= YYYY-MM-DDT23:59:59+00:00' which works because stored created_at also uses UTC offset format, but this is fragile string comparison — better: parse to datetime and use proper $lte with datetime object.

routes/transactions.py:87 — update_transaction accepts arbitrary dict (no Pydantic model), so clients can set forbidden fields like capital_applied, org_id, user_id. Consider an UpdatableFields pydantic model or whitelist.
seed_data_creation: One ephemeral TEST satis user created+torn-down inside pytest fixture for 403 tests. One transaction created+permanently-deleted during tx log tests. Activity logs persisted (audit trail by design).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 44 backlog fully validated. Reusable regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter44.py (export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL before running). Note: login endpoint returns 'token' not 'access_token' — fixtures should accept either. The DigestPanel localStorage key bug was fixed in this iter. Scheduler runs on Monday 09:00 Europe/Istanbul cron; verified by startup log only (real 7-day wait not testable).
rca_of_the_issue: Frontend DigestPanel used wrong localStorage key 'token' instead of app-wide 'crm_token'. Repro: login as admin → Settings → click Önizleme or Test Gönder → 401 'Invalid token' rendered in digest-result block. Fix: single-char replace 'token' → 'crm_token' at DigestPanel.jsx:15. Verified post-fix: preview iframe opens and send-now returns Resend email_id.
